heading: Definitions
label: 5031 Definitions
text: For the purposes of this chapter, a “juvenile” is a person who has not attained his eighteenth birthday, or for the purpose of proceedings and disposition under this chapter for an alleged act of juvenile delinquency, a person who has not attained his twenty-first birthday, and “juvenile delinquency” is the violation of a law of the United States committed by a person prior to his eighteenth birthday which would have been a crime if committed by an adult or a violation by such a person of section 922(x).
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 921 (, ).June 16, 1938, ch. 486, § 152 Stat. 764The phrase “who has not attained his eighteenth birthday” was substituted for “seventeen years of age or under” as more clearly reflecting congressional intent and administrative construction. The necessity of a definite fixing of the age of the juvenile was emphasized by Hon. Arthur J. Tuttle, United States district judge, Detroit, Mich., in a letter to the Committee on Revision of the Laws dated . Words “an offense against the” was changed to “the violation of a” without change of substance.June 24, 1944Minor change was made in translation of section references to “this chapter”.

Amendments1994— inserted before period at end “or a violation by such a person of section 922(x)”.Pub. L. 103–3221974— amended section generally, inserting “or for the purpose of proceedings and disposition under this chapter for an alleged act of juvenile delinquency, a person who has not attained his twenty-first birthday” after “eighteenth birthday,” and substituting “committed by a person prior to his eighteenth birthday which would have been a crime if committed by an adult”, for “committed by a juvenile and not punishable by death or life imprisonment.”Pub. L. 93–415
Repeals, , , cited as a credit to this section, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 93–415, title V, § 50188 Stat. 1133Pub. L. 115–385, title III, § 307132 Stat. 5152Sept. 7, 1974Dec. 21, 2018
